How to handle refunds. Everyone's favorite topic. So, well, with everyone, I like to give some best practice. So, first, and I mean this genuinely, like, if you feel like, like if someone signs a 12-month contract, you don't have them for 12 months. You have them for today. And so, you have to prove to them that it's worth, you know, stay in contracts if you feel like you, if they feel like you're not holding up your side of the bargain. So, yesterday, that's it. You're only good as your last workout for these people. Great service and you deliver it all the way through and once they're members as well because they're there. Second, if someone hits the goal, all right, simple as that. If someone did not hit the goal and they want their money back, explain to them, I would ask one question before that just saying like, the satisfaction guarantee is based on whether you feel like we delivered poor service. Do you feel like we gave you bad service? Do you feel like the trainers give you bad service? Which trainer do you feel like gave you poor service? If they're like, oh, okay, well then, so you basically want to walk them through that conversation and see if there's anything else you can do for them to make them satisfied. But at the end of the day, if someone wants their money back and is upset, give them their money back. If someone did not finish, let's say someone did two weeks and then says, you know what, this isn't for me. I want my money back. I would usually say, do you feel like it's fair to at least compensate us for this, you know, for the period of time that you were here, which would be, you know, at a normal rate would be $100 or whatever for the two weeks or three weeks, whatever it is that you were here. A lot of times they're like, yeah, that sounds fair. Then you can refund the remainder. Most of that people are crazy and it's a numbers game. So if you get 100 sales, you're going to have some people back out. Period. You're going to have some people be crazy people. Like if you thought you could take a random sampling of 100 people in the United States, like what percentage, it's not crazy for crazy people to do crazy things. It's crazy for same people to expect crazy people to do same things. All right, so don't be crazy, right? Expect crazy people to do crazy things. So if someone disappears and then comes back from the dead, what I mean by that is like they, you get no communication with them, then like, you know, two months after their challenge is over or they call you saying they want to refund. In those situations, I try and use discernment, try and use reason. But at the end of the day, with all of these situations, if someone really wants the money back, we give them their money back. All right, so I want you to adopt that. I always like, you always want to be reasonable and see if there are things that you can do. Well, why don't we just get you started again? Like asking the main questions like, have you lost weight yet? Like we'll forget about the last one, the last six weeks, even though you did two weeks of it, we'll just start you over again, free charge on me. Right? Like I want to help you out. And you should mean that, which you should. And sometimes a lot, sometimes those people will come back and do fine. All right, so try and try and go alternate routes. But if this person just seems like crazy, crazy situation that isn't your fault, I'm pregnant, my car crashed, which is like, definitely don't sign up for a gym. Because if you do that, the likelihood that like, someone in your immediate family like gets cancer, or you're in a car accident, or you get pregnant, you're like quadruples. And that's because people lie, and they just use excuses. And you can try and like, actually find logic of their attention. It takes you an annoyance of having these people on your lines, on your own, your attention lines of communication. It's just not worth dealing with them. It makes you fall out of love with your, your existing clients. It makes you see prospects in the world a different way. And you don't want that. So find amicable solutions that I would be able to serve them without doing that. And if they still thought that way, I gave them the money back. All right, it's just not worth it. Because people can still charge back, there's a money, a million things they can do. And so ultimately, in this day and age, the consumer has the power. So we'll give them their money back. All right. And when you do that, it's very hard for anyone to hold anything against it. It's like, I gave them my money back. We don't want to do it. So, and just as a reminder, one of the reasons that this makes, you know, the offer on the front end, so we're resistable is that we offer money back guarantee. And it should be a satisfaction money back guarantee. So the satisfaction is on the level of service they receive. And so I just like to remind them, you know, what part of the service like who who failed you so I can know. And usually, you know, they won't, they won't fit, you know, you, they, your body didn't respond in a certain way. Well, I'm kind of upset about that. Okay. Well, and then you got to reinforce like, okay, so you made progress, you didn't hit 25, but you hit 13. That's still amazing. It's more than you did in the past. So you have to say, they want to refund and then after five minutes of just venting, tell me what you still want to lose weight. Yeah, well, then let's do it. You know what I mean? Like, I'll give you the next week on me. Like, don't worry, let's just get started. I'll see you tomorrow night at six. All right. Cool. You know what I mean? And you can bring a man and and handle it that way. You can show this to your operations manager to your team so they understand serve them to give them a refund in terms of helping them in the long term. So actually try and serve them. If all else fails, then by all means, give them the refund. Don't try and hold it hostage. It's not the right way to do business. All right. Lots of love and I'll see you soon.
